rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE M. HASBUN ET AL VS. 101 LOMBARD STREET CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of settlement on file. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROSEMARIE MACGUINNESS ET AL VS. 246 CARL STREET (EAT), LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT MERCURY ENGINEERING GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is moot. On May 2, 2022 Plaintiff filed a Notice of Settlement. On May 10, 2022 Plaintiff presented an unopposed ex-parte application for DOE Amendment Only (Ex-parte 1:25-26) re-asserting that a settlement was reached with all other parties (Ex-parte 2:4). On May 12, 2022 the Court issued an order granting leave to amend the complaint limited to the DOE amendment. The Court was not asked to and did not rule on a continued litigation against parties in the Notice of Settlement. The Court notes that Mercury Engineering is not named as a Doe Defendant in the First Amended Complaint and the Court could not locate any mention on Mercury Engineering in the body of the First Amended Complaint. The Court further notes that Plaintiff changed the caption of the case without leave of Court. Therefore, the First Amended Complaint in its present form, filed without leave of Court is stricken. Plaintiff shall have until December 9, 2022 to either (1) file an amended complaint clearly identifying Mercury Engineering as a DOE defendant and being the sole defendant in this case; or (2) to file a motion for leave to file an amended complaint re-naming the existing defendants (in light of the Notice of Settlement) and naming Mercury Engineering as a Doe. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARK SMITH VS. JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Arlene Henry'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum; And Mister Phillips' Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC AND ARLENE HENRY'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of causes of action as to defendant Arlene Henry for untenantable dwelling, breach of contract and UCL violation. The Court is further inclined to reconsider its decision to sustain the demurrer to the cause of action for breach of warranty of habitability. If parties do not contest this portion of the tentative ruling, it will become the order of the Court. If parties contest this portion of the ruling, the Court will set a hearing and allow for supplemental briefing as to the sole issue of reconsidering the Court's decision to sustain the demurrer "without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of warranty of habitability. This is an affirmative defense to an unlawful detainer action." =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NOZARI 2, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. J.P. MORGAN CHASE, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Modify Order Granting Defendant Jpmorgan Chases Motion For Order Of Reference Regarding Paying Party And Agreed Upon Referee, Or Alternatively, To Vacate Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 1, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION TO MODIFY ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE'S MOTION FOR ORDER OF REFERENCE REGARDING PAYING PARTY AND AGREED UPON REFEREE, OR ALTERNATIVELY, TO VACATE ORDER is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Modify the Order regarding the agreed upon referee is GRANTED. Judge Lynn O'Malley-Tailor shall be the referee is she is still available to conduct the proceedings. Defendant to present an amended ADR-110 fir execution no later than December 5, 2022. If judge O'Malley-Taylor is no longer available, parties shall either (1) provide a joint declaration no later than December 5, 2022 regarding an agreed upon new referee along with an amended ADR-110; or (2) each submit a declaration providing 3 names of proposed referees no later than December 5, 2022. Motion is otherwise denied. Issues regarding adhesion, payment of fees, unconscionability, etc., were to be addressed and were necessarily decided on the motion for reference. The Court notes that the opening brief was 15 pages long and contained to Table of Contents or Table of Authorities, therefore, the last 5 pages of the brief are considered and stricken. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
